title: Maximum Number of Eaten Apples
description:
There is a special kind of apple tree that grows apples every day for n days. On the ith day, the tree grows apples[i] apples that will rot after days[i] days, that is on day i + days[i] the apples will be rotten and cannot be eaten. On some days, the apple tree does not grow any apples, which are denoted by apples[i] == 0 and days[i] == 0.

You decided to eat at most one apple a day (to keep the doctors away). Note that you can keep eating after the first n days.

Given two integer arrays days and apples of length n, return _the maximum number of apples you can eat._

solutionCode.python:
```python
def unhappyFriends(n, preferences, pairs):
    pair_mapping = [0] * n
    for pair in pairs:
        pair_mapping[pair[0]] = pair[1]
        pair_mapping[pair[1]] = pair[0]

    unhappy_count = 0
    for x in range(n):
        y = pair_mapping[x]
        pref_x = preferences[x]
        idx_y = pref_x.index(y)

        for i in range(idx_y):
            u = pref_x[i]
            v = pair_mapping[u]
            pref_u = preferences[u]
            if x in pref_u[:pref_u.index(v)]:
                unhappy_count += 1
                break

    return unhappy_count // 2
```

    
    1. Initialize a pair_mapping array to store the pairings given in pairs.
2. Loop through each friend (denoted by x in the for loop) from 0 to n.
3. Check the y position in x's preference list.
4. Loop through friends 0 to y position in x's preference list (excluding y).
5. For each friend u, who x prefers more than y, check if u prefers x over its paired friend v.
6. If u does prefer x over v, increment the unhappy_count and break the loop.
7. Once all friends are checked, return half the value of the unhappy_count, as each unhappy friend is counted twice.
